nodes.label: friend | lament | time
nodes.sentence: A friend of mine once lamented about the hard times he was living through.

nodes.label: he | tell | i
nodes.sentence: He told me he had a small income and a large family, without the strength to handle the burden of poverty.

nodes.label: he | think | ∅
nodes.sentence: Often, he thought about moving to another country so that, no matter how he lived, no one would know whether his fortune was good or bad.

nodes.label: he | say | ∅
nodes.sentence: He said many people sleep hungry, and no one even knows who they are.

nodes.label: ∅ | lie | death
nodes.sentence: Many lie near death, but no one mourns for them.

nodes.label: he | choose | comfort
nodes.sentence:
He was also worried about his enemies, who would mock him behind his back, accusing him of struggling for his family out of weakness rather than strength, and saying:

“Look at that dishonorable man who will never 
Know prosperity’s face,
He chooses comfort for himself,
Leaving his wife and children to suffer.”

nodes.label: he | ask | i
nodes.sentence: He also asked me, since I knew he had some skill with numbers, if I could use my influence to get him a position.

nodes.label: this | ease | mind
nodes.sentence: This would ease his mind and make him indebted to me, a debt he could never repay with simple thanks.

nodes.label: i | answer | ∅
nodes.sentence: I answered, “My friend, jobs under a king come with two things: hope for bread and risk to life.

nodes.label: man | say | ∅
nodes.sentence: Wise men say it's unwise to take such risks just for hope.”

nodes.label: one | visit | home
nodes.sentence:
No one visits a dervish’s home
To demand taxes on his fields and gardens.

nodes.label: you | accept | worry
nodes.sentence:
You must either accept your worries and sorrows
Or leave your beloved children to the crows.

nodes.label: he | answer | ∅
nodes.sentence: He answered, “You have not really addressed my situation or answered my question.

nodes.label: honesty | be | way
nodes.sentence:
Have you not heard the saying, ‘Whoever betrays shall tremble when judged’?”

Honesty is the way to God’s favor.

nodes.label: i | see | ∅
nodes.sentence: I have never seen anyone lost who walked the straight path.

nodes.label: adulterer | fear | informer
nodes.sentence: The wise say, “Four types of people live in fear of four others: a robber fears the sultan, a thief fears the watchman, an adulterer fears the informer, and a harlot fears the overseer.

nodes.label: ∅ | be | power
nodes.sentence:
But who fears when their conscience is clear?”

Do not be wasteful while in power, if you want
Your enemies to be embarrassed when you leave.

nodes.label: ∅ | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: Be pure, brother, and fear no one.

nodes.label: washerman | beat | clothe
nodes.sentence: Washermen only beat dirty clothes on stones.

nodes.label: i | say | ∅
nodes.sentence: I said, “Your story reminds me of a fox I heard about.

nodes.label: it | say | ∅
nodes.sentence: It was running with great fear and when asked why, it said, ‘I heard camels are being forced into service.’

nodes.label: people | say | ∅
nodes.sentence: People said, ‘Fool, what do you have to do with camels?

nodes.label: you | resemble | one
nodes.sentence: You don’t resemble one.’

nodes.label: fox | reply | ∅
nodes.sentence: The fox replied, ‘Quiet!

nodes.label: who | care | ∅
nodes.sentence: If my enemies maliciously call me a camel and I get caught, who will care to help or defend me?

nodes.label: person | die | ∅
nodes.sentence: By the time a cure comes from Iraq, the person bitten by the snake dies.’

nodes.label: you | be | man
nodes.sentence: You are an excellent, honest man, but enemies lie in wait everywhere.

nodes.label: you | force | ∅
nodes.sentence: If they twist your character, you’ll be forced to explain yourself to the king and face blame.

nodes.label: who | dare | ∅
nodes.sentence: Who dares speak on your behalf then?

nodes.label: i | think | ∅
nodes.sentence: So, I think you should retreat into contentment and give up ambitions for power.

nodes.label: wise | say | ∅
nodes.sentence:
The wise have said:”

“There are many treasures in the sea,

nodes.label: ∅ | stay | shore
nodes.sentence: But if you want safety, stay on shore.”

nodes.label: friend | grow | ∅
nodes.sentence: My friend grew angry when he heard this.

nodes.label: he | frown | ∅
nodes.sentence: He frowned and scolded me, saying, “What kind of wisdom is that?

nodes.label: ∅ | call | friend
nodes.sentence:
The philosophers’ words prove true—friends are useful only in prison, for when dining, all enemies act like friends.”

Do not call a friend one who knocks on your door
Only when you are prosperous,
Boasting of friendship and calling himself your brother.

nodes.label: i | call | friend
nodes.sentence:
I call a friend one who takes the hand of a friend
When he is poor and distressed.

nodes.label: i | go | president
nodes.sentence: Seeing he had changed and accused me of speaking out of self-interest, I went to the President of the State Council.

nodes.label: i | explain | situation
nodes.sentence: Trusting my old friendship with him, I explained my friend’s situation, and he gave my friend a small position.

nodes.label: nature | earn | praise
nodes.sentence: Soon my friend’s good nature and management earned praise, and he was promoted to a higher office.

nodes.label: fortune | rise | ∅
nodes.sentence: His fortune rose steadily until he reached the peak of his hopes, becoming a courtier to the sultan, well-respected and trusted.

nodes.label: i | feel | ∅
nodes.sentence:
I felt happy for his stable position and said:

“Do not fear complicated troubles or carry a broken heart,
For life’s spring hides in darkness.”

Do not grieve, brother in hardship,
For the Merciless hides favor in shadows.

nodes.label: fruit | be | ∅
nodes.sentence:
Do not sit gloomy over life’s changes;
Though patience is bitter, its fruit is sweet.

nodes.label: i | go | pilgrimage
nodes.sentence: Later, I went on a pilgrimage to Mecca with friends, and on the way back, my friend met me two stages away, looking troubled and dressed like a dervish.

nodes.label: i | ask | ∅
nodes.sentence: I asked, “What happened?”

nodes.label: he | say | ∅
nodes.sentence: He said, “As you predicted, some envied me and accused me of treason.

nodes.label: king | check | ∅
nodes.sentence: The king did not even check if it was true.

nodes.label: supporter | forget | bond
nodes.sentence: My old supporters, kind friends who should have spoken the truth, forgot all our past bonds.

nodes.label: you | see | ∅
nodes.sentence:
“Don’t you see how before a man in power,
People bow and praise him,
But when fortune turns,
They all trample on his head?

“For weeks, I suffered persecution, until news arrived of pilgrims returning from Mecca.

nodes.label: i | free | chain
nodes.sentence: That day, I was freed from my heavy chains, but my inherited property was taken.”

nodes.label: i | reply | ∅
nodes.sentence: I replied, “You didn’t listen when I said serving kings is like sailing the sea—profitable but dangerous.

nodes.label: you | gain | treasure
nodes.sentence: You either gain treasure or are lost in the waves.”

nodes.label: gold | throw | he
nodes.sentence:
The official either grabs gold with both hands on shore,
Or the waves one day throw him dead onto the shore.

nodes.label: you | know | ∅
nodes.sentence:
Not wanting to increase my friend’s pain or rub salt into his wounds, I simply recited these two lines:

“Did you not know your feet would be in chains
If you cannot hear the advice of others?

nodes.label: ∅ | put | finger
nodes.sentence:
And if you cannot stand the sting’s pain,
Do not put your finger inside a scorpion’s hole.”
